channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I'm About to Deploy My Go + React App for the First Time — Wish Me Luck
item.description: Here's the stack: Backend: Go, Gin, JWT auth, PostgreSQL (hosted on Neon.tech), Cloudinary for image uploads Frontend: React + Tailwind CSS I haven't actually clicked deploy yet. This post is me...

item.title: Flawed humans, perfect machines: A new era of self-awareness.
item.description: Our digital existence is shifting rapidly as we enter an era where technology ceases to be merely a tool in our hands and transforms into an entity with which we coexist daily. Personal AI is no...

item.title: Why Every ASP.NET Core Developer Should Learn Clean Architecture
item.description: As applications grow, so does complexity. Features become harder to add, debugging takes longer, and changing one module unexpectedly breaks another. One of the most effective ways to solve this...

item.title: Your Hand-Typed Slop Isn't Honest. It's Just Slower.
item.description: A post on X last week: "The fact that people can't even reply to posts without AI anymore says a lot more about them than they think." The replies agreed. Nobody asked the obvious question: says...

item.title: Decorators Are Just Functions. Here Is Proof.
item.description: Decorators Are Just Functions. Here Is Proof. The @ symbol in Python is syntax sugar. That is the entire secret to understanding decorators. Strip away the syntax sugar and trace exactly what...

item.title: Self-hosting an x402 facilitator: the EIP-3009 gotchas that almost cost us money
item.description: We run DeskCrew, a helpdesk where AI agents can use support tools with no account and no API key — they pay per action in USDC over x402, the HTTP-402 payment standard. An agent calls a tool, gets...

item.title: Closures and Scope in JavaScript: Lexical Scope, Closures, and IIFE Explained
item.description: When learning JavaScript, one of the most important — and sometimes confusing — concepts you’ll encounter is scope and how it interacts with closures. These ideas determine how variables are...

item.title: "This code isn't working yet,..."
item.description: A small collection of the all-to-often exclamations of software developers: "This code isn't working (as expected) yet,..." 1, (classic) "it works on my machine." "it worked fine yesterday." "I...

item.title: downbeat: stop copy-pasting between your AI terminals
item.description: Context I run more than one AI coding session at a time (Claude Code, in my case). One is usually the planner: it holds the architecture, decides what to do, splits the work. The others are...

item.title: Using D365 Customer Insights for Our Own Sales Team (Customer Zero) (2) Building Segments
item.description: Customer Insights - Data Configuration Now that data unification is complete, it's time to configure D365 Customer Insights - Data. From the left menu, go to Insights → Segments and click the...

item.title: Meta's New AI Image Tool Lets Others Use Your Public Instagram Photos in AI Images
item.description: Meta has announced that its new artificial intelligence (AI) model Muse Image lets people use public Instagram posts and reels to generate AI content, and it's enabled by default. "You can also...

item.title: Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
item.description: Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead. That is the finding in a proof-of-concept published Wednesday...

item.title: GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
item.description: Researchers at Wiz found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. The assistant asks permission to edit one...

item.title: Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure...

item.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
item.description: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

item.title: Meta is reportedly working on smart glasses that would be recording all the time
item.description: Meta might be the next company to make an always-on AI wearable. The company is working on prototype "super sensing" always-aware smart glasses that could continuously record audio and snap photos...

item.title: Messi and Ronaldo Are Building Tech Portfolios. Mo Salah Is Playing a Different Game
item.description: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo are betting on AI, health tech, and startups. Mohamed Salah is taking a more traditional route beyond football.

item.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
item.description: Earlier this week, a picture seemed to show Kentucky Senator Mitch McConnell covered in tubes in a hospital bed in a state of extreme distress. It turned out to be an AI-generated fake.

item.title: I Built a Self-Improving AI, and So Can You
item.description: Experiments in using AI to build AI show that the future doesn’t just belong to the frontier labs.

item.title: Get a $30 credit when you reserve Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy phones
item.description: Even though they haven’t been officially announced yet, Samsung is giving you a chance to save some cash when you preorder what we’re expecting to be the brand’s updated Galaxy Z Fold phones. The...

item.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
item.description: Elon Musk's tech company released the newest version of Grok on Wednesday, promising a cheaper, more efficient alternative to other powerful AI models.

item.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
item.description: General Intuition is betting millions of hours of video game data can train the foundation models for physical AI, making it easier to build smarter robots with minimal real-world data.

item.title: Google pays $250K for Linux vulnerability allowing guest VM escapes
item.description: Both vulnerabilities allow untrusted users to gain root privileges.

item.title: Microsoft’s Xbox reset is pivoting Obsidian to make Fallout instead of Avowed
item.description: As part of Microsoft's big Xbox "reset," which includes layoffs affecting 3,200 staffers, jettisoning studios, and shifting investments to focus on "higher priority projects," Obsidian...

item.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
item.description: The feature can do things like apply cinematic relighting to brighten up a dark clip, swap out a plain background for something fun, or add artistic styles to videos.

item.title: Aussie gov't tells volunteers to throw out thousands of functioning test routers
item.description: But the devices could "easily be reflashed."

item.title: America’s cheapest new EV is smaller than a ping-pong table and tops out at 19mph
item.description: When searching for an affordable electric vehicle these days, there are always tradeoffs. How much range are you willing to sacrifice, how much leg room and storage space, how many features, in...

item.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
item.description: When it comes to achieving artificial general intelligence (AGI), large language models just don’t have what it takes. Models like ChatGPT and Claude are great at text, but they’re less skilled at...

item.title: Cockroaches will learn to fear my SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable
item.description: A little robotic switch-flipper has become my sidekick in combating cockroaches. Before I got the SwitchBot Bot Rechargeable, I'd tiptoe through the dark every morning, hoping I wouldn't step on...

item.title: Data for Agents
